parity_type: rustscenic vs pyscenic GRN on identical PBMC 3k fixture

fixture.preprocessing: scanpy.datasets.pbmc3k() + filter_cells(min_genes=200) + filter_genes(min_cells=3) + normalize_total(1e4) + log1p — baked into rustscenic-ref:0.12.1 Docker image at /data/pbmc3k.h5ad
fixture.source: scanpy.datasets.pbmc3k (the canonical PBMC 3k bundled with scanpy)

configuration.pyscenic_call: arboreto.algo.grnboost2(expression, tf_names=tfs, seed=777, client_or_address=None) inside rustscenic-ref:0.12.1 Docker image

honest_assessment: Per-edge Spearman 0.611 on 480,680 shared edges — strong rank-correlation between rustscenic and pyscenic outputs.
Within-TF Spearman mean 0.632 (median 0.649) across all 1,274 shared TFs — coarse biology agrees.
Top-K edge Jaccard is intrinsically low (~0.15-0.34) for stochastic GBM pipelines with independent RNG streams; this is not a quality gap but a property of the algorithm class.

rustscenic emits 1.14M edges vs pyscenic's 0.95M — rustscenic retains slightly more low-importance edges before its importance>0 cutoff. Top-K agreement saturates well below n_edges so this does not affect downstream usage.
Speedup 1.78× on 10-core M5 (rustscenic 214s vs pyscenic sync 381s). pyscenic with dask-parallel would be faster than sync mode; not a strict apples-to-apples runtime comparison.

reference_image.rebuild_required: Yes — original Dockerfile pinned python:3.11-slim which currently resolves to 3.11.13 and broke dask 2024.1.1 via inspect.signature drift. v0.3.10 update pins python:3.11.7-slim to keep the reference reproducible.
